assertedBy: OpenEvo Computational Curriculum Studies Working Group
rationale: OE-INTERDISCIPLINARY's Evolutionary Process (OE-CONCEPT-000090, "any process by which variants are generated, selected, and transmitted across iterations within a system") shares a structural pattern with AI4K12's "Nature of Learning (Learning from experience)" row (OE-COMPETENCY-000395, 3-A-vi): both describe an iterative process that narrows down a space of possibilities toward better-performing variants over repeated trials -- reinforcement learning's "hundreds of thousands of games" of trial and error is structurally reminiscent of generations of selection acting on heritable variation. This is deliberately recorded as skos:relatedMatch rather than skos:closeMatch: reinforcement learning's trial-and-error search and biological/cultural selection are not the same mechanism (reinforcement learning has no analogue of heritable variation between "generations" of a single learning run, and its "reinforcement signal" is an externally-defined objective, not an emergent consequence of differential persistence). The LPM's own performance indicator (OE-STRAND-000214, 9-12) asks students to articulate exactly this genuine-analogy-vs-genuine-difference distinction; this alignment record is the evidentiary check for that claim, and its matchType level (relatedMatch, not closeMatch) is itself part of the answer -- the analogy is real but looser than a casual reading of "algorithmic selection" might suggest, and AI4K12 does not cover genetic algorithms specifically at all, only reinforcement learning's related but distinct trial-and-error structure.
subject: OE-INTERDISCIPLINARY-v1.0.0:OE-CONCEPT-000090
